description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched hip roof with deep overhangs, providing substantial shade to the structure. The exterior walls are finished in a light color, likely stucco or painted concrete, and the base is accented with a darker trim, possibly stone or brick. The front facade includes a prominent covered porch supported by sturdy columns, creating a welcoming entryway. Windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical pattern along the sides and rear, with the front porch partially obscuring the main entrance windows. The building sits on a circular patch of grass, suggesting a landscaped yard or garden area. The overall design is simple and functional, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation, characteristic of modern or ranch-style bungalows.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide hip roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement, finished in light-colored walls with dark trim. The structure sits on a landscaped circular lawn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch with columns and the main entrance, with the wide hip roof extending over the entry.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the plain back wall with minimal window openings and the roof's overhang.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of windows and the extended roof eaves.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows and the continuation of the roof's deep overhang.
